google.interviewer_personality: Collaborative and curious. Google interviewers often explore multiple solutions and care deeply about algorithmic elegance. They ask 'can you do better?' frequently.
common_follow_ups: What if the input doesn't fit in memory?
Can you parallelize this?
What's the best possible time complexity for this problem?
red_flags: Jumping to code without discussing approach
Unable to optimize beyond brute force
google.unique_traits: Google uses a hiring committee — your interviewer doesn't make the final call. They care about 'Googliness' — intellectual humility and collaborative problem-solving.

meta.interviewer_personality: Fast-paced and direct. Meta interviewers expect you to move quickly. They care about working code that handles edge cases. Speed matters.
common_follow_ups: What edge cases are you handling?
Can you code the optimal solution directly?
How would you test this?
meta.unique_traits: Meta expects you to write code in a shared document (no IDE, no autocomplete). Practice without syntax highlighting.

amazon.interviewer_personality: Leadership Principles focused. Amazon interviewers weave behavioral questions into technical rounds. They ask 'tell me about a time when...' even during coding.
common_follow_ups: How does this solution scale to millions of users?
Tell me about a time you had to make a quick decision with incomplete data.
What's the trade-off here?
red_flags: Not connecting technical decisions to business impact
amazon.unique_traits: Amazon is the only FAANG that heavily evaluates Leadership Principles in every round. Prepare 2-3 STAR stories per principle.

apple.interviewer_personality: Detail-oriented and design-conscious. Apple interviewers care about code quality, naming conventions, and software design. They may ask you to write tests.
common_follow_ups: How would you test this?
What design pattern would you use here?
How would you name this variable in production?
apple.unique_traits: Apple interviews are team-matched — you interview with the actual team you'd join. Culture fit matters more than at other FAANGs.

microsoft.interviewer_personality: Methodical and structured. Microsoft interviewers like to see systematic problem decomposition. They often ask about object-oriented design.
common_follow_ups: How would you design the classes for this?
What about thread safety?
How would this work in a distributed system?
interview_format: As-Appropriate final round with senior leader
microsoft.unique_traits: Microsoft's 'As-Appropriate' (AA) round is with a senior leader who decides hire/no-hire. It combines technical depth with behavioral assessment.

bloomberg.interviewer_personality: Practical and systems-oriented. Bloomberg interviewers focus on real-world coding ability and understanding of financial systems. They value production-quality code.
common_follow_ups: How would you handle millions of messages per second?
What happens when this service goes down?
How would you monitor this in production?
red_flags: Writing code that only works for the happy path
No consideration of error handling or edge cases
bloomberg.unique_traits: Bloomberg interviews often include pair programming. They value C++ expertise and understanding of low-latency systems.

netflix.interviewer_personality: Senior and direct. Netflix only hires senior engineers, so interviews are fast-paced with high expectations. They care about culture fit and independent thinking.
common_follow_ups: What would you do differently if you had more time?
How would you make this decision without manager approval?
What's the simplest solution that works?
netflix.unique_traits: Netflix culture emphasizes 'freedom and responsibility'. They want engineers who can make independent decisions and communicate directly.

stripe.interviewer_personality: Practical and API-focused. Stripe interviewers give problems that resemble real work — building APIs, handling edge cases in payment systems, debugging production code.
common_follow_ups: How would you handle a race condition here?
What if this API is called 10,000 times per second?
How would you version this API?
interview_format: Onsite with 4 rounds including a debugging exercise
stripe.unique_traits: Stripe interviews include a unique 'bug squash' round where you debug real-ish code. Practice reading and fixing other people's code.

uber.interviewer_personality: Systems-minded and pragmatic. Uber interviewers focus on distributed systems, real-time processing, and handling scale. They appreciate practical trade-off discussions.
common_follow_ups: How would this work with millions of concurrent users?
What happens during a network partition?
How would you handle data consistency here?
uber.unique_traits: Uber problems often involve geospatial data, real-time systems, and matching algorithms. Study rate limiting and consistent hashing.

tiktok.interviewer_personality: Fast-paced and algorithm-heavy. TikTok/ByteDance interviewers emphasize pure algorithmic ability. Interviews may include multiple coding problems in one session.
common_follow_ups: Can you solve this in O(n)?
What if the array is sorted?
Here's a harder version of this problem...
tiktok.unique_traits: ByteDance/TikTok interviews are notoriously algorithm-heavy. You may get 2-3 problems per round. Speed and accuracy both matter.

goldman-sachs.interviewer_personality: Technical and detail-oriented. Goldman Sachs interviewers care about clean code, understanding of data structures, and sometimes financial domain knowledge.
common_follow_ups: What's the worst-case scenario?
How would you handle floating-point precision?
Walk me through your testing strategy.
goldman-sachs.unique_traits: Goldman's 'Super Day' is an intensive onsite with back-to-back interviews. Prepare for both technical and behavioral questions about teamwork and ethics.

linkedin.interviewer_personality: Collaborative and product-minded. LinkedIn interviewers like candidates who think about the user impact of their technical decisions.
common_follow_ups: How would this feature impact the user experience?
What metrics would you track?
How would you A/B test this?
interview_format: Onsite with 4 rounds including system design
red_flags: Pure algorithmic thinking without considering product impact
linkedin.unique_traits: LinkedIn (owned by Microsoft) values 'members first' thinking. Connect your technical solutions to user value.
